workflow.name: Generate and send AI newsletters with OpenAI, Tavily, Google Sheets and Gmail
workflow.description:
# 📨 AI Newsletter Automation Workflow


## 🚀 Brief Overview (How It Works)

This workflow automates the complete process of generating and sending AI-powered newsletters. It starts with a form submission where the user provides a brand name, website, and topic query. The system then fetches relevant news, analyzes the brand’s visual identity (colors & logo), and uses AI to generate structured newsletter topics and content. Finally, it converts everything into a beautifully styled HTML email, saves it to Google Sheets, and sends it to all subscribers via Gmail.

---
## Quick Setup Guide
👉 [Demo & Setup Video](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1ulOGWNL3cKe3y7tSKWNG2jlpL7dyiAgX/view?usp=sharing)
👉 [Sheet Template](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1hvB5Zif52eCLv_X7E_OifQv9OI5usn-CQ50-TsZTMQA/edit?usp=sharing)
👉 [Course](https://www.udemy.com/course/n8n-automation-mastery-build-ai-powered-enterprise-ready/?referralCode=2EAE71591D3BEB80F2CC)

---

## 🔑 Nodes of Interest

- **On Form Submission (Trigger)**
  - Entry point for brand details and query input

- **HTTP Request (Tavily API)**
  - Fetches latest news based on the query

- **Generate Draft Topics (AI)**
  - Creates newsletter title + 3 key topics using LLM

- **Split Topics**
  - Breaks topics into individual items for processing

- **Generate Newsletter Content (AI)**
  - Writes detailed content for each topic with sources

- **Merge Content Pieces**
  - Combines all sections into one dataset

- **AI Agent (Brand Analyzer)**
  - Extracts brand colors from website HTML

- **Information Extractor**
  - Extracts logo URL from brand website

- **Convert Newsletter to HTML (AI)**
  - Formats content into styled email HTML using brand theme

- **Google Sheets**
  - Saves generated newsletter drafts

- **Gmail Node**
  - Sends newsletter to all subscribers

---

## 🔐 What You’ll Need (Credentials)

- **OpenAI API Key**
  - Used for all AI content generation (GPT-4.1-mini)

- **Tavily API Key**
  - For fetching real-time news data

- **Google Sheets OAuth**
  - To store newsletter drafts & manage subscribers

- **Gmail OAuth**
  - To send emails to subscribers

---

## ⚙️ Recommended Settings & Best Practices

- Use **structured output parsers** to ensure consistent AI responses  
- Keep **topic count fixed (3)** for predictable flow  
- Limit newsletter length to **≤ 1000 words** for readability  
- Use **Split + Aggregate pattern** for scalable content generation  
- Maintain **strict prompt engineering** (system messages already optimized)  
- Always validate **real URLs for citations** (avoid hallucinations)  
- Use **Loop Over Items** for controlled email sending  
- Store drafts before sending (fail-safe logging)

---

## 🎯 Customization Ideas

- Add **weekly automation trigger** (Cron node) instead of manual form  
- Integrate **Notion / Airtable** instead of Google Sheets  
- Add **unsubscribe & email tracking (SendGrid / Mailchimp)**  
- Personalize emails using **user segmentation (AI-based)**  
- Add **multi-language newsletter generation**  
- Include **image generation (DALL·E / Stable Diffusion)** for visuals  
- Connect to **Telegram / WhatsApp bot** for distribution  
- Add **analytics dashboard** for open/click rates  

---

## 🏷️ Tags

AI Automation, n8n Workflow, Newsletter Automation, Email Marketing, GPT Integration, LangChain, Tavily API, No-Code AI, Content Generation, Marketing Automation, GenAI Workflow, AI Agents

---

options.systemMessage:
# Overview
You are an expert newsletter planner. You will receive three articles from the past week. Your job is to come up with a creative and fun title as well as the main topics for this newsletter.

## Instructions
The newsletter should flow nicely, feel informative, and holistic. The topics should be 5-6 words.

=# Overview
You are a professional newsletter section writer. Your only task is to write one standalone section of a newsletter.

## Instructions
- Always include a clear section heading followed by the section content.
- Do not write an overall title, introduction, or conclusion.
- Write in a professional, expert, and engaging tone suitable for a business newsletter.
- If you reference facts, data, or quotes, you must cite your sources and provide the actual clickable URLs.
- Do not invent citations—only include real, verifiable sources.
- Keep the section concise, well-structured, and easy to read.

=You are an expert newsletter editor and visual designer. Merge exactly three provided sections into a cohesive, email-ready HTML body that maintains a consistent professional layout, while adapting only the text colors according to the provided brand color theme. 🎯 Objectives Refine clarity, coherence, and tone across all sections. Maintain a friendly, modern, Gen Z–aligned voice that’s professional yet approachable. Only text colors (headings, paragraphs, links, footer text) should dynamically adapt to the provided color theme. Preserve the original layout, structure, padding, and overall visual hierarchy exactly as provided. Ensure strong readability, balance, and flow. Keep total content ≤ 1000 words (hard limit). Avoid filler or redundant text. End every email with a friendly, brand-colored footer. 🎨 Brand Text Color Adaptation Rules Use the color theme study provided by the workflow, which typically includes: Variable Description primary_color Highlight / heading color secondary_color Body and paragraph text color accent_color Links and buttons background_color Overall background tone ✅ Important: Only apply these to text elements. Do not alter layout properties such as background, borders, padding, or container structure. 🧱 Fixed Layout Template

{{ $('On form submission').item.json['Brand Name'] }} Newsletter ✨

Hi {{ $json.Name }},

© {{ $now.format('yyyy') }} {{ $('On form submission').item.json['Brand Name'] }} | Built with 💚 for our community
Stay curious. Stay creative. 🚀

🖋 Element Styling (Only Text Color Adapts) Element Style

font-size:21px; color: {{ $('Aggregate').item.json.output[1].output.primary_color }}; margin-top:28px; margin-bottom:12px; font-weight:600;

font-size:18px; color: {{ $('Aggregate').item.json.output[1].output.secondary_color }}; margin-top:22px; margin-bottom:10px; font-weight:500;

font-size:16px; margin-bottom:18px; color: {{ $('Aggregate').item.json.output[1].output.secondary_color }};

    margin:18px 0 18px 25px; font-size:16px; color: {{ $('Aggregate').item.json.output[1].output.secondary_color }};
  • margin-bottom:10px; color: {{ $('Aggregate').item.json.output[1].output.accent_color }}; text-decoration:none; (hover underline optional) The layout (margins, padding, shadows, alignment) remains static. Only text colors are dynamically injected from the color study. 🧩 Content Structure Introduction Paragraph: Briefly introduce the three topics and mention today’s date: {{ $now.format('yyyy-MM-dd') }}. Each Section:

    : Use or slightly refine the section title.

    : Summarize clearly and engagingly. Add verifiable citations using clickable Source links. Sources Section:

    Sources:

    • [Publication Name] – [Article Title]
    Alphabetize by publication name Remove duplicates Conclusion: Summarize all three themes and end with an uplifting or call-to-action note. 🔗 Citation Rules Never fabricate or alter sources. Only use real, verifiable URLs. Standardize all sources into tags. Remove duplicates. 📥 Input You will receive: Three newsletter content sections Brand color theme study (primary_color, secondary_color, accent_color, background_color) Optionally: brand_name and brand_logo_url 📤 Output Format Subject line: One concise, specific subject (≤ 80 characters) Body: Fully styled HTML that applies dynamic text colors only, while preserving the fixed, professional layout and footer.

options.systemMessage:
analyze the website html data and then provide me the colour code this website is following like the theme of this website and output the result providing
primary_color – brand’s accent or highlight color

secondary_color – neutral contrast or typography color

accent_color – supporting tone for buttons or links

background_color – overall email background tone

parameters.content:
## Workflow Input (Form Trigger)
This section collects the required inputs from the user through a form submission.

## Extract Brand Style & Colors
This block analyzes the provided brand website to determine the brand’s visual identity.

## Fetch News About Query
This section retrieves news about the query

## AI Processing, HTML Generation & Email Delivery
This section handles the core content generation and delivery of the newsletter.
